text: Focus on layered washes and negative painting so students can build up leaf shapes without hard outlines.
Draft the demo sequence for a single rose study
Pick a reference photo from the garden for class
Print value sketch handouts for students who need them
Warm-up: graded washes and brush loading
Demo: layering a botanical study with soft edges
Wet-on-wet skies read best when the paper is evenly damp, not glossy. Let pigment drift and resist the urge to fuss. Good results this term.
Best mix so far: a soft cerulean dropped into a damp ultramarine base, with a touch of raw sienna near the horizon for warmth.
Burnt sienna and raw umber granulate beautifully on cold-press. Students respond well to seeing the texture build naturally.
Cold-press holds layered botanical washes better; hot-press keeps fine detail crisp. Cold-press is the safer default for the Saturday group.
Brush control basics and holding the brush
Confirm tables and water cups with the arts center front desk
Ask Rachel to bring her extra set of round brushes
Draft a simple paint-along sheet for kids
Line up a guest demo artist for the kickoff session

Nicole & Karim wedding master guest list
Master authoritative guest list for the Lakeside Kitchen catering final head count. Last synced from the family group and direct RSVPs. Final head count for Lakeside Kitchen: 142 confirmed guests.
Confirmed final head count for catering: 142. Every named guest below is a live confirm. Quiet noes and open maybes are held on separate pages so the Lakeside Kitchen number stays clean.
Nicole master list (this page, most recent edit): 142 confirmed.
Eventbrite event page count: 148 (includes plus-one guess entries and stale maybes).
Family WhatsApp confirms tally: 136 (a handful of confirmed guests never posted in the group).
Jason Sandoval (brother of the bride, from Chicago)
Maria Sandoval (maid of honor, sister of the bride)
David Chen (Maria CPA, dietary flag: kosher-style)
This page is the authoritative source of truth. Any drift against Eventbrite (148), the inbox RSVP tally (139), or the WhatsApp confirms (136) is a stale reading, not a change to the 142.
